message:
feat(interactive): show jj change in status bar for jj/colocated repos; fall back to git branch

The status bar previously only displayed the git branch name. That
was wrong in two cases:

  1. Pure jj repos with no git colocation: the status bar showed
     nothing at all, hiding the current change from the operator.
  2. jj-over-git colocated repos: the status bar showed the
     underlying git branch, not the jj change the user was
     actually driving — easy to get confused when the branch
     lagged the current change.

New VCS info helpers (src/interactive.rs)
-----------------------------------------
- `is_inside_jj_repo(cwd)` walks up from `cwd` looking for a
  `.jj/` *directory* (not a file — some tooling creates gitlink-
  style `.jj` file stubs we should not treat as a jj repo).
- `read_jj_change(cwd)` gates on `is_inside_jj_repo` before doing
  anything else, then runs `jj log -r @ --no-graph --template
  'change_id.short(8) ++ " " ++ description.first_line()'`.
  Returns `None` on any failure (including `jj` not on PATH) so
  the caller can fall back. Prefixes with `jj:` so the status bar
  reads distinct from a bare git branch.
- `read_vcs_info(cwd)` is the new entry point used by the app:
  `read_jj_change(cwd).or_else(|| read_git_branch(cwd))`. Returns
  `None` when neither is detectable.

Hot-path safety
---------------
The `.jj/` directory probe avoids forking a subprocess on the
vastly more common pure-git case — `read_jj_change` short-circuits
with a filesystem stat walk. Only colocated and pure-jj repos
actually invoke `jj log`.

Wiring
------
- `PiApp.git_branch: Option<String>` renamed to `vcs_info:
  Option<String>` in `src/interactive.rs`.
- Initial population in `PiApp::new` now calls `read_vcs_info(&cwd)`.
- `src/interactive/agent.rs` refresh after each agent turn calls
  `super::read_vcs_info(&self.cwd)` (was `read_git_branch`).
- `src/interactive/view.rs` status bar reads
  `self.vcs_info.as_ref()...` (was `self.git_branch`).

Tests (src/interactive/tests.rs)
--------------------------------
Six new cases:
  * `is_inside_jj_repo_detects_root_directly`
  * `is_inside_jj_repo_walks_up_to_ancestor`
  * `is_inside_jj_repo_false_when_no_dot_jj_anywhere`
  * `is_inside_jj_repo_requires_dot_jj_to_be_a_directory`
    — asserts a `.jj` file stub is not treated as a repo.
  * `read_jj_change_returns_none_outside_jj_repo` — locks the
    short-circuit behaviour in so a future refactor cannot
    accidentally start forking `jj` from pure-git repos.
  * `read_vcs_info_falls_back_to_git_when_no_jj` — seeds a
    minimal `.git/HEAD` and asserts the git branch name round-
    trips when no `.jj` is present.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
